title: [InstCombine] Always treat inner and/or as bitwise

tests.source_program:
define <2 x i1> @vector_bitwise_and_logical_and_icmps(<2 x i8> %x, <2 x i8> %y, <2 x i8> %z) {
  %c1 = icmp eq <2 x i8> %y, <i8 42, i8 42>
  %x.m1 = and <2 x i8> %x, <i8 1, i8 1>
  %z.shift = shl <2 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1>, %z
  %x.m2 = and <2 x i8> %x, %z.shift
  %c2 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m1, zeroinitializer
  %c3 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m2, zeroinitializer
  %and1 = select <2 x i1> %c2, <2 x i1> %c1, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %and2 = and <2 x i1> %and1, %c3
  ret <2 x i1> %and2
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define <2 x i1> @vector_bitwise_and_logical_and_icmps(<2 x i8> %x, <2 x i8> %y, <2 x i8> %z) {
  %c1 = icmp eq <2 x i8> %y, <i8 42, i8 42>
  %x.m1 = and <2 x i8> %x, <i8 1, i8 1>
  %z.shift = shl <2 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1>, %z
  %x.m2 = and <2 x i8> %x, %z.shift
  %c2 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m1, zeroinitializer
  %c3 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m2, zeroinitializer
  %and1 = select <2 x i1> %c2, <2 x i1> %c1, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %and2 = and <2 x i1> %and1, %c3
  ret <2 x i1> %and2
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary does not mention vector types at all. All existing tests use scalar types (i1, i8, i32). This test uses <2 x i1> and <2 x i8> vector types, which are outside the documented scope but should still be handled by the parameterized reassociateBooleanAndOr function.

alive2.src:
define <2 x i1> @vector_bitwise_and_logical_and_icmps(<2 x i8> %x, <2 x i8> %y, <2 x i8> %z) {
  %c1 = icmp eq <2 x i8> %y, <i8 42, i8 42>
  %x.m1 = and <2 x i8> %x, <i8 1, i8 1>
  %z.shift = shl <2 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1>, %z
  %x.m2 = and <2 x i8> %x, %z.shift
  %c2 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m1, zeroinitializer
  %c3 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m2, zeroinitializer
  %and1 = select <2 x i1> %c2, <2 x i1> %c1, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %and2 = and <2 x i1> %and1, %c3
  ret <2 x i1> %and2
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <2 x i1> @vector_bitwise_and_logical_and_icmps(<2 x i8> %x, <2 x i8> %y, <2 x i8> %z) {
  %c1 = icmp eq <2 x i8> %y, splat (i8 42)
  %z.shift = shl nuw <2 x i8> splat (i8 1), %z
  %x.m2 = and <2 x i8> %x, %z.shift
  %c2 = trunc <2 x i8> %x to <2 x i1>
  %c3 = icmp ne <2 x i8> %x.m2, zeroinitializer
  %and1 = select <2 x i1> %c2, <2 x i1> %c1, <2 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %and2 = and <2 x i1> %and1, %c3
  ret <2 x i1> %and2
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @pure_bitwise_inner_or_outer_or(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
  %m1 = and i8 %x, 1
  %c1 = icmp ne i8 %m1, 0
  %m2 = and i8 %y, 2
  %c2 = icmp ne i8 %m2, 0
  %inner_or = or i1 %c1, %c2
  %m3 = and i8 %z, 4
  %c3 = icmp ne i8 %m3, 0
  %result = or i1 %inner_or, %c3
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @pure_bitwise_inner_or_outer_or(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
  %m1 = and i8 %x, 1
  %m2 = and i8 %y, 2
  %m3 = and i8 %z, 4
  %combined = or i8 %m1, %m2
  %combined2 = or i8 %combined, %m3
  %result = icmp ne i8 %combined2, 0
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Similar to test 1 but for OR operations. The existing tests focus on logical OR (select-based), but this tests purely bitwise OR inner operations which the patch handles through the unified reassociateBooleanAndOr helper.

alive2.src:
define i1 @pure_bitwise_inner_or_outer_or(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
  %m1 = and i8 %x, 1
  %c1 = icmp ne i8 %m1, 0
  %m2 = and i8 %y, 2
  %c2 = icmp ne i8 %m2, 0
  %inner_or = or i1 %c1, %c2
  %m3 = and i8 %z, 4
  %c3 = icmp ne i8 %m3, 0
  %result = or i1 %inner_or, %c3
  ret i1 %result
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @pure_bitwise_inner_or_outer_or(i8 %x, i8 %y, i8 %z) {
  %m1 = and i8 %x, 1
  %m2 = and i8 %y, 2
  %1 = or disjoint i8 %m1, %m2
  %m3 = and i8 %z, 4
  %2 = or disjoint i8 %1, %m3
  %result = icmp ne i8 %2, 0
  ret i1 %result
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @eq_comparison_zero_mask_reassociation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
  %m1 = and i32 %a, 255
  %c1 = icmp eq i32 %m1, 0
  %m2 = and i32 %b, 255
  %c2 = icmp eq i32 %m2, 0
  %inner_and = and i1 %c1, %c2
  %m3 = and i32 %c, 255
  %c3 = icmp eq i32 %m3, 0
  %result = and i1 %inner_and, %c3
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @eq_comparison_zero_mask_reassociation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
  %m1 = and i32 %a, 255
  %m2 = and i32 %b, 255
  %m3 = and i32 %c, 255
  %combined = or i32 %m1, %m2
  %combined2 = or i32 %combined, %m3
  %result = icmp eq i32 %combined2, 0
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: This uses icmp eq with zero comparisons instead of icmp ne with non-zero. The existing tests focus on ne/0 patterns, but the patch's reassociation logic applies to eq/0 patterns as well through foldBooleanAndOr.

alive2.src:
define i1 @eq_comparison_zero_mask_reassociation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
  %m1 = and i32 %a, 255
  %c1 = icmp eq i32 %m1, 0
  %m2 = and i32 %b, 255
  %c2 = icmp eq i32 %m2, 0
  %inner_and = and i1 %c1, %c2
  %m3 = and i32 %c, 255
  %c3 = icmp eq i32 %m3, 0
  %result = and i1 %inner_and, %c3
  ret i1 %result
}
